name: Hanging Temple of Hengshan
description: A fifth-century temple improbably built into the side of a cliff.

name: Avatar Hallelujah Mountain
description: This vertical pinnacle in China was the inspiration for the floating mountains in the movie “Avatar.”

name: Red Beach
description: A part of the world's largest marsh seems to be growing bright red Martian grass.

name: Houtouwan
description: This abandoned fishing settlement may well be the greenest village in the world.

name: The Forbidden City
description: The most famous location in China was home to whole dynasties worth of imperial rulers.

name: Tianmen Shan
description: A total of 999 stairs lead visitors through a massive hole in a mountain known as the Gateway to Heaven.

name: Huanglong Travertine Pools
description: Intensely colorful calcite pools in Southern China.

name: Zhangye National Geopark
description: These layered sandstone mountains in China look like they are made of pressed crayons.

name: Bund Sightseeing Tunnel
description: This psychedelic tourist trap is a leisurely descent into madness.

name: Great Wall of China Slide
description: Why walk on this historical site when you can zoom down instead?

name: Luoxiagou
description: A canvas of crops grow from vibrant red soil in this terraced farmland.

name: Yangzhou Zhongshuge
description: Mirrored floors and arched shelves create the illusion of an infinite tunnel of books.

name: Tianmen Skywalk
description: For those with no fear of heights: a skywalk 4,700 feet up on a Chinese mountain.

name: Shilin Stone Forest
description: 270 million year-old forest of stone.

name: Three Natural Bridges
description: Not one, not two, but three natural bridges remain in this captivating spot post-sinkhole.

name: Zhangjiajie Glass Bridge
description: The latest in a series of long glass bridges suspended at terrifying heights.

name: China Watermelon Museum
description: This Chinese museum proves that it's a watermelon world, we're all just living in it.

name: Fengdu Ghost City
description: The ancient settlement attracts tourists from all over looking to learn about the Chinese vision of the afterlife.

name: Huashan Temple
description: A sacred mountaintop temple offers a fine cup of tea to visitors who can survive the journey.

name: Harbin Ice and Snow Festival
description: One of the world's largest ice festivals dazzles visitors for over a month with radiant sculptures and carvings.

name: Honghe Hani Rice Terraces
description: A complex and beautiful system of rice terraces covers a million acres over the mountains.

name: Giant Buddha
description: This Tang Dynasty behemoth is the biggest Buddha in the world.

name: Jiuzhaigou Valley
description: Snow-capped peaks, colorful waterfalls and stunning vistas on the edge of the Himalayas.

name: Old Dragon's Head
description: Where the Great Wall of China meets the sea.

name: Yangshuo and Moon Hill
description: Natural arch offers panoramic views of Yangshuo's karst hills.

name: Yungang Grottoes
description: Explore this massive series of 45 caves and over 51,000 sculptures carved from cliffsides in China.

name: Maijishan Grottoes
description: Rock-cut caves and 7,000 Buddhist sculptures carved into the nearly sheer face of a sandstone mountain.

name: Booming Dunes of Badain Jaran Desert
description: The tallest stationary dunes on Earth also happen to sing.

name: Ming Dynasty Tombs
description: Ming Dynasty necropolis raided during the Cultural Revolution.

name: Tianjin Binhai Library
description: China’s breathtaking futuristic library is lined floor to ceiling with fake books.

name: Guoliang Tunnel
description: A tunnel dug through the side of a mountain by hand provides convenient yet death-defying access to a small village in China.

name: Shanghai Propaganda Poster Art Centre
description: An collection of Chinese propaganda posters from 1949 to 1979.

name: 798 Art District
description: This probably wasn't what Mao had in mind.

name: Yuncheng Salt Lake
description: Rainbow-hued in the summer and crystalline in the winter, this salty lake has inspired battles, temples, and plenty of awe.

name: Shimao Wonderland Intercontinental Hotel
description: The world's first hotel built in an abandoned water-filled quarry.

name: World Joyland Theme Park
description: This incredible Chinese theme park may be the largest bootleg in history.

name: Destination Club
description: A progressive LGBTQ hub offers dancing, HIV testing, art, and more.

name: Shanghai's 1933 Slaughterhouse
description: Rivers of cattle blood gone, a strange Escheresque  building remains, the last of its kind.

name: Beijing Ancient Observatory
description: This ancient Chinese observatory still contains early astronomy technology.

name: Fanjingshan (Mount Fanjing)
description: Two ancient Buddhist temples crown this dramatic rocky pinnacle.

name: Haohan Qiao — "Brave Men's Bridge"
description: At nearly 1,000 feet long, this astonishing glass suspension bridge is one of the longest in the world.

name: Blue Moon Valley
description: A bright turquoise river cascades beautifully down stepped terraces in southern China.

name: East Taihang Glasswalk
description: This terrifying glass bridge on the side of a mountain cracks as you step on it.

name: Buxian Bridge
description: This short pedestrian span is also known as the "Fairy Walking Bridge" because it looks almost magical.

name: Dongyue Temple
description: This sensational Chinese temple walks visitors through the 76 weird departments of the afterlife.

name: Beijing Capital Museum
description: Home to over 200,000 artifacts, this museum attempts to guide visitors through the ancient history of Beijing and Imperial China.

name: Longyou Caves
description: Discovered after draining a local pond, these ancient man-made caves are still a complete mystery.

name: The World Park
description: Beijing's World Park offers the excitement of international sites without the travel.

name: Western Han Dynasty Terracotta Warriors Museum
description: These tiny statues form a terracotta army from China's Han dynasty.

name: Bailong Elevator
description: The world’s tallest outdoor lift stretches along the stone pillars that inspired the floating mountains in the movie “Avatar.”

name: Fangweng
description: A cliff-hanging restaurant.

name: Chinese Pyramids of Xian
description: Enormous burial mounds of ancient emperors.

name: Reed Flute Cave
description: Hidden behind musical flora and forgotten for over a thousand years, this cave is now a neon dream.

name: Erlian Kissing Dinosaurs
description: The largest dinosaurs in the world, kissing above the highway.

name: Dinosaurs Fairyland
description: Jurassic theme park in the small town of Erlian.

name: Huashan Mountain
description: A harrowing trail up a mile-high mountain.

name: Crescent Lake
description: A lush desert oasis in the shape of a narrow crescent.

name: Working People's Cultural Palace
description: This smaller cousin of the Forbidden City has gone from ancestral sacrifice to tourist's whispers.

name: Oriental Pearl Tower
description: The once highest point in China blends symbolic design with a kick of adrenaline.

name: Yelang Valley
description: It took an artist 20 years to create this sprawling sculpture garden honoring the area's ancient inhabitants.

name: Shaolin Flying Monks Theatre
description: A magical work of architecture where a wind turbine enables monks to levitate.

name: Shanghai Marriage Market
description: A son-and-daughter meat market, hawked by mom and dad.

name: Shi Cheng
description: China's very own Atlantis is an ornate dynastic city resting at the bottom of a lake.

name: Longmen Grottoes
description: The field of tiny caves that mark this Chinese cliffside are each their own historic Buddhist temple.

name: Shanghai French Concession
description: An out-of-place slice of France, smack in the middle of Shanghai.

name: Paleozoological Museum of China
description: A bisected journey through evolution's history in the heart of Beijing.

name: St. Regis Lhasa Resort Swimming Pool
description: Described as "the world's most blinging pool."

name: Shanghai Circus World
description: China's most famous acrobatics act.

name: Tianzi Hotel
description: Where better to stay the night than inside a trio of ancient Chinese deities representing "the good life."

name: Mogao Caves
description: Hundreds of caves filled with thousands of centuries-old statues and murals.

name: Giant Head of Mao Zedong
description: The world's largest statue of Mao Zedong looks out over the city where he discovered Communism.

name: Unit 731 Museum
description: An impactful museum ensures notorious war crimes disguised as "medical experimentation" won't soon be forgotten.

name: Heicheng Ruins
description: Sited in an Inner Mongolian desert are the remnants of an important trade stop along the Silk Road.

name: M50 Art District
description: This former textile district now is now a thriving center for contemporary Chinese art.

name: The Great Thangka
description: Stretching almost 2,000 feet, this painting is one of the longest pieces of Buddhist artwork in the world.

name: Window of the World
description: 130 replicas of world wonders squeezed into just 118 acres of space.

name: Bai Gong Fang (100 Handicraftsmen Workshop)
description: A studio devoted to keeping hundreds of unique Chinese arts alive.

name: Submerged Great Wall of China
description: Thanks to the building of a life-giving reservoir, part of the Great Wall of China is now a dive site.

name: Rongbuk Monastery and Guesthouse
description: The highest monastery in the world operates a tiny guesthouse with doors open to all.

name: Drigung Monastery
description: This Tibetan monastery has survived multiple attacks and is also a sky burial site.

name: The Fushan Tunnels
description: Forgotten German bunkers in China create a sharp cultural contrast.

name: Mansudae Art Studio Gallery
description: North Korea's massive art and propaganda factory now has an official gallery space in Beijing.

name: The Qingping Market
description: A fantastic assortment of China's finest and most disturbing wares.

name: Scale Model of Shanghai
description: A giant scale model giving a sneak peek at what urban planners see for Shanghai by 2020.

name: Chime-Long Ocean Kingdom
description: This aquarium has almost as many world records as it does fish.

name: The Fox Tower at Dongbianmen
description: This historic Chinese tower was said to have been haunted by fox spirits but is now haunted by a grisly murder.

name: Mount Danxia
description: Unique geological formations in China, featuring some suggestive shapes.

name: Spring Temple Buddha
description: The largest statue in the world towers at 420 feet high atop another 66 feet of pedestal.

name: The Eye of Heaven
description: The largest radio telescope in the world searches for extraterrestrial life from the remote limestone peaks of southern China.

name: Hailan Horse Culture Museum
description: A palatial horse museum outside Shanghai is home to the most pampered horses in the world.

name: Shanghai Film Park
description: This film studio and amusement park is considered one of the greatest filming locations in China.

name: Fujian Tulou Cluster
description: Walled family complexes in Southern China.

name: 816 Underground Nuclear Plant
description: This top secret Chinese military megaproject is the world’s largest human-made tunnel structure.

name: Mati Si
description: Monks turned this mountain into a temple by digging grottoes, tunnels, and a huge pagoda-like hall.

name: Sidu River Bridge
description: This highest bridge in the world spans such a great chasm that it had to be established using rockets.

name: Shanghai Natural History Museum
description: This ratty natural history collection is a charming relic among a city rushing into modernity.

name: Lovers in the Mist
description: Thousands of locks professing undying devotion line the metal chains of Lotus Peak.

name: Tianjin Eye
description: This massive neon wheel is the only one of its kind to be built over a bridge.

name: Peace Hotel Jazz Bar
description: This bar is home to what's likely the oldest living jazz band in the world.

name: Dazu Rock Carvings
description: Comprised of thousands of cave temple carvings this cornucopia of ancient Chinese stone art is unrivaled anywhere in the world.

name: Matryoshka Square (Russian Taowa Square)
description: Hundreds of large Russian nesting dolls decorate this square, and the largest is a functioning hotel.

name: The Amazing Robots of Wu Yulu
description: A chinese farmer builds robots from scrap.

name: Du Fu KFC
description: A KFC honors an eighth-century Chinese poet with holograms and verse.

name: Canola Flower Fields
description: This quiet area of Yunnan Province annually transforms into an unbelievable sea of yellow flowers.

name: The Giant Buddha of Guifeng
description: China's mysterious record-setting sleeping Buddha.

name: West Xia Imperial Tombs
description: China's beehive-shaped pyramids house the remains of a lost kingdom.

name: Huaqing Pools
description: These mountain hot springs have seen power struggles, doomed romances, and a generalissimo's attempted escape from mutiny.

name: Tuyoq
description: Natives of this mud-brick desert village use an ancient irrigation system to grow sweet, seedless grapes.

name: 1930 Folk Street
description: This tunnel looks like a sliver of Shanghai that's stuck in the past.

name: Guangfulin Relics Park
description: This theme park presents 5,000 years of Shanghai history and includes a "sunken" exhibition hall.

name: Mystery Lakes of the Badain Jaran Desert
description: No one is exactly sure why over a hundred lakes have formed amongst the tallest sand dunes on Earth.

name: Da Shuhua
description: A spectacular annual festival centers around humans throwing molten iron against a wall at night.

name: Jiaohe Ruins
description: The ancient remains of this natural fortress can still be found along the Silk Road looking surprisingly preserved.

name: Kangbashi
description: It seems that the residents of of this megalopolis suddenly vanished, but they were never actually there.

name: Hotan Sunday Market
description: This weekly market in the remote oasis city of Hotan still has the atmosphere of the old Silk Road.

name: Shipton's Arch (阿图什天门)
description: The world's largest natural arch was forgotten for over 50 years after its discovery.

name: Hallstatt, China
description: A Chinese mining company has created the world's first cloned village in this perfect recreation of an Austrian town.

name: Jade Burial Suits
description: Stone shrouds of Prince Liu Sheng and Princess Dou Wan of the Han Dynasty, created to preserve their flesh for eternity.

name: Tianducheng
description: In its continuing quest to recreate famous parts of the world in miniature, China now has its own ghost Paris.

name: Jiayuguan Fortress
description: The western-most fortress on the Great Wall of China has gone from a strategic stronghold to a kitschy tourist carnival.

name: Shanghai Jewish Refugees Museum
description: A wall within the institution displays the names of 13,000 Jews who took refuge in the city during World War II.

name: Chairman Mao Memorial Hall
description: The embalmed remains of the communist leader lie within a custom crystal coffin.

name: Sword of Goujian
description: This ancient sword lay in a waterlogged tomb for 2,500 years, but managed to remain relatively untarnished and impressively sharp.

name: Tiger Leaping Gorge
description: One of the word's deepest gorges is also tied to a fantastic legend.

name: Fazangjiang Temple
description: This under-visited temple is nestled within one of Shanghai's traditional shikumen-style residential blocks.

name: Guo Li Zhuang
description: Genitals, claws, and faces are served at this Beijing eatery.

name: Beijing Ancient Architecture Museum / Xiannongtan (Temple of Agriculture)
description: An ancient Chinese temple where even the emperor dressed like a farmer in the name of the harvest.

name: Chinese Printed Blue Nankeen Exhibition Hall
description: A small gallery and shop dedicated to an ancient style of hand-printed textile.

name: Confucian Temple of Shanghai
description: A prestigious learning institute of old Shanghai.

name: Bozhou Medicinal Herb Market
description: The largest traditional Chinese medicine market in the world sells ingredients that may make you squirm.

name: Underground Project 131
description: This Cold War–era underground city includes offices for Mao Zedong and Lin Biao.

name: Golden Summit Temple
description: A golden temple atop a sacred mountain, known for its giant effigy of the revered Pu Xian.

name: Librairie Avant-Garde
description: With former lives as a bomb shelter and government parking lot, this shop is one of China’s most extraordinary locales for book-lovers.

name: Maritime Silk Road Museum
description: Inside this museum is an ongoing excavation of an ancient shipwreck.

name: Tian Yi's Tomb
description: The burial grounds of a Chinese eunuch who served his way into the highest levels of Imperial government.

name: Li Jiawan Grand Ginkgo King
description: The world's largest ginkgo tree contains a hollow so large a man once lived in it for two years.

name: Zigong Dinosaur Museum
description: China's monumental dinosaur museum featuring an open dig site.

name: Huaxi Village
description: The so-called "Richest Village In China" is either a model Communist town or a dystopian sham.

name: New South China Mall
description: One of the world's largest malls has been almost completely empty since its opening.

name: Xi'an City Walls
description: An ancient fortification lives on in this Chinese city.

name: A.P. Xinyang Market
description: A labyrinth of clothing, electronics, and other counterfeit items.

name: Anji Bamboo Museum
description: Home to around 40 different species, this forest was also the setting for "Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon."

name: Guangzhou Circle
description: The world's tallest circular building is loaded with symbolism, but many just see a big donut.

name: Luohanyuan Shuangta (Twin Pagodas)
description: Two nearly identical Chinese pagodas stand side by side, along with ancient stone relics.

name: The Seven Cats of Shantang Street
description: Thanks to a bit of wordplay, seven auspicious stone cats guard this ancient canalside street.

name: Whitewater Village Scenic Area
description: Man-made waterfall is the tallest in Mainland China.

name: Tanggula Railway Station
description: At 16,627 feet above sea level, this unstaffed station is the highest in the world.

name: White Horse Temple (白马寺)
description: This temple located on a vertical drop of a rocky mountain houses relics from the Song Dynasty.

name: Tarim Mummies
description: These excavated burials from the Tarim Basin are wrapped in mystery.

name: Guan Yu Statue
description: China has many statues of the general and war god, but none are as epic as this.

name: Baigong Pipes
description: Are these prehistoric metal pipes evidence of alien plumbers? Nope, but they are amazing nonetheless.

name: Qin Shan Zhai
description: Every dish at this restaurant claims to have a health benefit.

name: Dafen Oil Painting Village
description: The knock-off oil painting capital of the world.

name: London Tower Bridge Replica
description: The Chinese have long been excellent at the art of copying, but this epic replica takes the biscuit.

name: Kashgar Livestock Market
description: Be careful not to get trampled at this lively two thousand-year-old market.

name: Guiyu
description: China's biggest e-waste village.

name: Afaq Khoja Mausoleum
description: An exquisite example of Uyghur Islamic architecture.

name: iBox
description: A collection of cafes and shops all crafted from recycled storage containers.

name: Duge Beipanjiang Bridge
description: The highest bridge in the world rises a dizzying 1,854 feet above the river.

name: Site of the First National Congress of the Chinese Communist Party
description: The powerful party began with a secret meeting in this shikumen in 1921.

name: Beichuan Earthquake Museum
description: A museum and memorial built amid the rubble of a town obliterated by nature.

name: General Tso's Tomb
description: The man who is mainly remembered for a chicken dish he had nothing to do with is buried under a large concrete dome.

name: Huanghuagang Mausoleum of the 72 Martyrs
description: A replica of the Statue of Liberty watches over the tomb of 72 Chinese revolutionaries.

name: Great Mosque of Xi'an
description: Although resembling a grand Buddhist temple, this structure is actually the largest mosque in China.

name: Abandoned China Medical University
description: Forgotten for decades, the first medical school built in Communist China is full of surprises today.

name: Nanjie Village
description: One of China's last communal villages wears its ideals on its sleeve.

name: Eurasian Pole of Inaccessibility
description: Eurasia's most difficult place to hang out, and farthest point from sea access.

name: Yan Huang Plaza
description: 350-foot-high emperor faces in China's Yellow River valley.

name: Star Islands of Qian'an
description: Though these human-made islands were constructed as an escape for the wealthy, they rarely see many visitors.

name: Chair of Bod
description: Large rock high in the mountains of Yunnan that is naturally shaped like a chair, complete with arms.

name: Chongyuan Temple's Guanyin Statue
description: An enormous effigy of the goddess of compassion hides within the sanctuary.

name: Foraminifera Sculpture Park
description: Unusual outdoor collection of sculptures.

name: Ring of Life
description: The massive steel ring looks like it could be China’s first Stargate.

name: Tomb of Sa'ad Ibn Abi Waqqas
description: A shrine to the missionary who first brought Islam to China.

name: Bo'ao
description: Small Chinese fishing village impeccably maintained for visitors.

name: Liebian International Building
description: On special occasions, one of the world's tallest artificial waterfalls gushes down the side of this Chinese skyscraper.

name: Precious Belt Bridge
description: This bridge dates back to the 9th century and allows visitors to walk an ancient path amid modern industrial trappings.

name: Labrang Meditation Huts
description: These picturesque structures speckle the hill above the monastery.

name: Ceramic Pillows at the Museum of the Nanyue King's Mausoleum
description: Three floors above a 2,000-year-old royal tomb is a rare collection of ancient porcelain pillows.

name: Longhua Martyrs Cemetery
description: A memorial to those who died at the hands of the Kuomintang while fighting for communism in China.

name: Alluvial Fan of the Taklimakan Desert
description: Made by river sediment, it is the largest alluvial fan in the world.

name: The Silver Dragon - Qiantang River Tidal Bore
description: A tidal bore with its own festival.

name: China Nantong Abacus Museum
description: The world's foremost museum dedicated entirely to the abacus.

name: Ceramic House
description: A clay house decorated with ceramic chips in Tianjin.

name: The Temple and Cemetery of Confucius
description: The family compound and final resting place for the philosopher, Confucius, and over 100,000 of his descendants.

name: Ruiguang Tower
description: The earliest pagoda built in the beautiful Panmen Scenic Area of Suzhou.

name: Xuanwu Lake Park
description: Once an imperial lake garden, anyone can now visit this city park.

name: White Cloud Temple
description: First established in 741, this is one of the oldest Taoist temples in China.

name: Cizhong Village
description: This Tibetan and Naxi village's Honey Rose wine bears the mark of 19th-century French missionaries.

name: Rainbow Bridge
description: Nova reconstruction of the bridge that baffled engineers for years.

name: Kaiping Dialous
description: Massive fortified towers blend North American and Chinese architecture.

name: Yumen Ghost Town
description: This once-booming oil city is now a relic of the region's industrial past.

name: Kanas Lake
description: A lake that borders Kazakhstan and Russia allegedly harbors the giant "Terror Trout" or "Chinese Nessie."

name: Dr Sun Yat-sen’s Former Residence
description: The birthplace of modern China.

name: Longhua Temple
description: A temple complex has existed on this site since the third century.

name: Terracotta Army of Qin Shi Huang
description: A life-sized imperial army, buried in battle formation, has stood underground for over 2,000 years—built not for war, but for eternity.

name: Zhou Enlai's Former Residence
description: The home where the first Premier of the People's Republic of China worked and lived.

name: Ming Xiaoling Mausoleum
description: One of the largest, grandest imperial mausoleums was built as the founder of the Ming Dynasty's final resting place.

name: Red Wave Valley Danxia Landform
description: A fascinating example of the Danxia landform is characterized by its striking red sandstone formations.

name: Lu Xun Memorial Park
description: The histories of modern Chinese literature and anti-Japanese resistance collide in this Shanghai park.

name: ZJNU Museum of African Culture
description: A museum at a Chinese university is the first of its kind in the country.

name: Zhang Heng Seismoscope
description: A replica of the first seismoscope ever invented.

name: Shimao Relics
description: A massive ancient Chinese city with stone walls and evidence of human sacrifices.

name: Bingling Temple
description: A hidden Buddhist gem accessible only by boat.

name: Tongzi Dan: Boy Eggs
description: Urine-soaked Chinese eggs.

name: Shaolin Monastery
description: The real Buddhist temple where the fictional Kwai Chang Kaine (David Carradine) trained in TV's 'Kung Fu' series.

name: Yan'an News Memorial Hall
description: A quiet ode to Communist China's propaganda machine, including a newspaper edited by Mao Zedong's own hand.

name: Guiyuan Temple
description: Count the statues to gain insight into your future from Buddhist disciples.

name: Tomb of Nie Er
description: The memorial gravesite of the young composer of China's national anthem.

name: Mount Kailash Kora
description: A pilgrimage around a forbidden peak sacred to four faiths.

name: Map of Pingjiang
description: You can still find your way around Suzhou with this 800-year-old stone map.

name: Shibaozhai
description: This majestic twelve-story wooden pagoda built into the side of a divine hill was saved from the Three Gorges Dam.

name: Foding Palace
description: A massive underground palace containing a colossal reclining Buddha statue and the only Buddha parietal bone relic in the world.

name: Yiwu International Trade City
description: Buy wholesale almost anything imaginable at what may be the world’s largest small-commodities market.

name: Qin Hui Statue at Yue Fei Temple
description: Visitors take revenge upon the statue of a traitor made to kneel at the tomb of his enemy.

name: Geodetic Intersection Point
description: A marker in suburban Hangzhou shows the intersection of the 30th parallel north and the 120th meridian east.

name: Dragon Escalator
description: The world's longest outdoor escalator takes visitors to the top of China's largest dam and is also shaped like a dragon.

name: The Central Perk, Beijing
description: This working replica of the 'Friends' coffee shop is more accurate to the show than the set itself.

name: Dixia Cheng - Beijing's Underground City
description: Beijing's expansive subterranean hiding spot now crumbles away as a tourist destination.

name: China's Wonderland
description: Beijing's version of Disneyland, completely abandoned, mostly destroyed.

name: Professor Dalin's Museum of Sex
description: 9,000 years of carnal history is on display at China's original Museum of Sex.

name: Q Confucius Number 2
description: Lifelike statue of the Chinese philosopher in the bathtub.

name: Flask and the Press
description: Leave the wardrobes and sweets to British schoolchildren, Shanghai's got a Coke machine that leads to booze.

name: Toilet Bowl Waterfall
description: What can compare to the comforting sound of 10,000 toilets running?

name: Shanghart Supermarket
description: This supermarket in Shanghai appears to be a typical Chinese convenience store, but all of the bottles are empty and all the containers are filled with nothing but air.

name: Heima Bar
description: A Viking-themed bar in a canal town for those retreating from the hustle and bustle of Shanghai.
